case_studies.title: Buffett's Coca-Cola Investment (1988)

thesis.moat: World's strongest brand, global distribution network, customer loyalty
thesis.valuation: Bought at 15x earnings when market feared saturation
thesis.management: Roberto Goizueta focused on shareholder returns
thesis.outcome: Position grew to $23B, 15x return over decades

lessons: Great brands can command pricing power for decades
Global distribution is a durable competitive advantage
Don't sell wonderful companies even when 'fully valued'
Patience with high-quality businesses pays off

case_studies.title: Buffett's See's Candies (1972)

thesis.moat: Regional brand loyalty, premium positioning, gift-giving tradition
thesis.key_insight: Discovered that customers would pay more each year
thesis.outcome: Generated $2B in cumulative profits on $25M investment

lessons: Pricing power is the ultimate test of a moat
Business with pricing power can compound without capital reinvestment
Owner earnings matter more than reported earnings
Regional brands can have durable moats

case_studies.title: Klarman's Distressed Investing Approach

thesis.strategy: Buy assets at deep discounts during market panic
thesis.catalyst: Wait for mean reversion or corporate action

lessons: Asset value provides floor, even in worst case
Patience is required - positions may take years to work
Cash is a strategic asset for opportunistic deployment
